title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a beach safety course.
audio_script: Woman: Hello, everyone. I’m pleased you’ve signed up for our beach safety course. It is useful for anyone who spends time near the sea, especially families and young people who enjoy water sports. The course will be held at [14]South Beach[/14], not at the swimming pool, because we want you to practise in a real outdoor place. We’ll meet beside the lifeguard hut at nine o’clock. Please be on time, as the tide changes quickly in the morning. The first topic will be how to understand [15]flags[/15]. These tell swimmers where it is safe to go into the water and when they should stay out. After that, you’ll learn what to do if someone gets into trouble. You won’t have to swim far, but you should bring a [16]towel[/16] and dry clothes. There are changing rooms near the café, but they can get busy. At lunchtime, we’ll have a break in the [17]clubhouse[/17]. You can buy food there, or bring your own. In the afternoon, we’ll practise using a simple rescue board. The instructor for this part is Luke [18]Miller[/18], who has worked as a lifeguard for twelve years. The course normally costs fifteen pounds, but students pay [19]eight[/19] pounds if they show their school card. At the end, everyone will receive a small booklet to take home. Please check the weather forecast before you come, and wear sun cream even if it looks cloudy. Parents are welcome to stay and watch from the beach wall, but they cannot join the practical part of the course. If the wind is strong, some activities may move to the classroom beside the hut.

questions.explanation: The course will be held at South Beach.
The first topic will be how to understand flags.
Students should bring a towel and dry clothes.
The lunch break will be in the clubhouse.
The afternoon instructor is Luke Miller.
Students pay eight pounds with a school card.
